{{Short description|2025 film festival in Italy}} {{Infobox film festival|name=82nd Venice International Film Festival|image=82nd Venice Film Festival poster.jpg|caption=Official poster by Manuele Fior|opening=''[[La grazia]]''|closing=''[[Dog 51]]''|location=[[Venice]], [[Italy]]|founded=1932|awards=[[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Father Mother Sister Brother]]''|host=[[Emanuela Fanelli]]|date=27 August – 6 September 2025|website={{URL|https://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema}}|chronology=Venice Film Festival|preceded_by=[[81st Venice International Film Festival|81st]]|followed_by=83rd|artistic_director=[[Alberto Barbera]]}} The '''82nd annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''' was held from 27 August to 6 September 2025, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]]. American filmmaker [[Alexander Payne]] served as Jury President for the main competition,{{Cite web |date=2025-04-28 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Alexander Payne President of the Venezia 82 international jury |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/alexander-payne-president-venezia-82-international-jury |access-date=2025-04-28 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} while Italian actress and comedian [[Emanuela Fanelli]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{Cite web |date=2025-07-01 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Emanuela Fanelli to host the opening and closing nights of Venezia 82 |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/emanuela-fanelli-host-opening-and-closing-nights-venezia-82 |access-date=2025-07-03 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[Father Mother Sister Brother]]'' by [[Jim Jarmusch]].{{Cite web |last=Tartaglione |first=Nancy |date=2025-09-06 |title=Venice Film Festival – Jim Jarmusch’s ‘Father Mother Sister Brother’ Is Golden Lion Winner; ‘Voice Of Hind Rajab’ Takes Grand Jury Prize; Benny Safdie Best Director For ‘The Smashing Machine’ |url=https://deadline.com/2025/09/venice-film-festival-2025-awards-winners-full-list-1236510204/ |access-date=2025-09-06 |website=Deadline |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |date=2025-09-06 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Official awards of the 82nd Venice International Film Festival |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/official-awards-82nd-venice-international-film-festival |access-date=2025-09-07 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} German filmmaker [[Werner Herzog]] and American actress [[Kim Novak]] were awarded the [[Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement]] during the festival.{{Cite web |date=2025-04-08 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Werner Herzog Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/werner-herzog-golden-lion-lifetime-achievement |access-date=2025-04-28 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}}{{Cite web |date=2025-06-09 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Kim Novak Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/kim-novak-golden-lion-lifetime-achievement |access-date=2025-06-09 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} Before and during the festival numerous [[Palestinian nationalism|Pro-Palestine]] demonstrations were held, supported by the festival organization or by stars attending,{{Cite web |author1=Rebecca Rubin |author2=Alex Ritman |date=2025-08-30 |title=Huge Gaza Protest Marches Toward Venice Film Festival as Thousands Attend Rally |first=Melanie |date=2025-08-25 |title=Gaza Set To Dominate Venice As August 30 March Gains Momentum & Activists Call For Gerard Butler & Gal Gadot Invites To Be Withdrawn |url=https://deadline.com/2025/08/gaza-venice-august-30-march-gerard-butler-gal-gadot-1236496630/ |access-date=2025-09-03 |website=Deadline |language=en-US}}{{Cite news |last=Khomami |first=Nadia|date=2025-08-27 |title=Activists hope to shift Venice film festival focus on to crisis in Gaza |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/aug/27/activists-hope-to-shift-venice-film-festivals-focus-on-to-crisis-in-gaza |access-date=2025-09-03 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}{{Cite news |date=2025-08-30 |title=Pro-Palestinian Protesters Raise Gaza War at the Venice Film Festival |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2025/08/30/arts/gaza-protest-venice-film-festival.html |access-date=2025-09-03 |language=en}}{{Cite web |date=2025-09-05 |title=Palestine takes centre stage at the Venice Film Festival |url=https://cineuropa.org/en/newsdetail/483126/ |access-date=2025-09-05 |website=Cineuropa - the best of european cinema |language=en}} specially during the world premiere of [[Gaza Strip|Gaza]]-set docudrama ''[[The Voice of Hind Rajab]]'', where further demonstrations took place demanding an end for the [[Gaza genocide|ongoing violence in Gaza]] during its 23-minutes long standing ovation.{{Cite web |author1=Nancy Tartaglione |author2=Nada Aboul Kheir |date=2025-09-03 |title=Gaza Drama ‘The Voice Of Hind Rajab’ Gets Record 23-Minute-Plus Ovation At Venice World |access-date=2025-09-03 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |last=Ritman |first=Alex |date=2025-09-03 |title=‘The Voice of Hind Rajab’ Stuns Venice With Its Longest Standing Ovation of 22 Minutes Amid Tears and ‘Free Palestine’ Chants |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/festivals/voice-of-hind-rajab-venice-premiere-standing-ovation-1236505878/ |access-date=2025-09-03 |website=Variety |language=en-US}} Artistic director [[Alberto Barbera]] endorsed any demonstrations, but stated that the protests should not disrupt the festival's schedule.{{Cite web |last=Dalton |first=Ben |title=Venice head Alberto Barbera says festival won’t disinvite guests for political views |url=https://www.screendaily.com/news/venice-head-alberto-barbera-says-festival-wont-disinvite-guests-for-political-views/5208265.article |access-date=2025-09-03 |website=Screen |language=en}}{{cite 2025}} The festival opened with ''[[La grazia]]'' by [[Paolo Sorrentino]],{{Cite web |date=2025-07-04 |title=Biennale Cinema 2025 {{!}} Paolo Sorrentino’ La Grazia is the Opening Film of the Biennale Cinema 2025 |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/paolo-sorrentino%E2%80%99-la-grazia-opening-film-biennale-cinema-2025 |access-date=2025-07-04 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} and |''Socks'' |Todd Karehana |- | colspan="2" |''A Very Good Boy'' |[[Samuel Te Kani]] |} === Venice Spotlight === Formerly called ''Orizzonti Extra'', the section was officially renamed ''Venice Spotlight'' in this year edition. The collateral section programs independent films with commercial appeal from newcomers filmmakers or internationally established ones. The section winner is attributed by popular vote at the end of the festival, the following films were selected: {| class="wikitable" ! scope="col" style="width:25%;" |English Title ! scope="col" style="width:25%;" |Original Title ! scope="col" style="width:25%;" |Director(s) ! scope="col" style="width:25%;" |Production Country |-style="background:#E7CD00;" | colspan="2" |'''''[[Calle Málaga]]''''' |'''[[Maryam Touzani]]''' |'''Morocco, France, Spain, Germany, Belgium''' |- |''[[Hijra (film)|Hijra]]'' |هجری |[[Shahad Ameen]] |Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Egypt, United Kingdom |- |''[[It Would Be Night in Caracas]]'' |''Aún es de noche en Caracas'' |[[Mariana Rondón]] and Marite Ugas |Mexico, Venezuela |- |''[[A Loose End]]'' |''Un Cabo Suelto'' |[[Daniel Hendler]] |Uruguay, Argentina, Spain |- | colspan="2" |''Made In EU'' |[[Stephan Komandarev]] |Bulgaria, Germany, Czech Republic |- | colspan="2" |''[[Motor City (film)|Motor City]]'' |[[Potsy Ponciroli]] |United States |- |''[[Silent Rebellion]]'' |''A Bras-le-Corps'' |Marie-Elsa Sgualdo |Switzerland, France, Belgium |- |''[[Tired of Killing: Autobiography of an Assassin]]'' |''Ammazzare stanca'' |[[Daniele Vicari]] |Italy |} === Venice Classics === Filmmaker Tommaso Santambrogio will serve as Jury President over 24 students recommended by professors of film studies from various Italian universities, the group will award the prizes for ''Best Restored Film'' and for the ''Best Documentary About Cinema''.
